questions.question: Which of the following best describes a robot?
options: A machine that looks like a human
A programmable machine capable of sensing, planning, and acting
questions.explanation: A robot is defined by its ability to sense its environment, plan actions, and execute them — not by its appearance.

questions.question: In the sense-plan-act cycle, what is the correct order of operations?
options: Sense the environment, plan a course of action, then act on it
Sense and act simultaneously without planning
questions.explanation: The classic sense-plan-act paradigm starts by perceiving the environment through sensors, then computing a plan, and finally executing actions through actuators.

questions.question: A robotic arm has 3 revolute joints, each rotating around a single axis. How many degrees of freedom (DOF) does it have?
questions.explanation: Each independent revolute joint contributes one degree of freedom. Three single-axis revolute joints give the arm 3 DOF total.

questions.question: Which of the following is an actuator rather than a sensor?
questions.explanation: A servo motor is an actuator — it produces motion. LIDAR, IMUs, and cameras are all sensors that gather information about the environment.

questions.question: An open-loop control system uses feedback from sensors to correct its output.
questions.explanation: Open-loop control does NOT use feedback. It executes commands without checking the result. Closed-loop (feedback) control measures the output and adjusts accordingly.
